methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Framework Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Whisper Aero is a Series A consumer tech company founded by Mark Moore and Ian Villa that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three private comparables—Shinkei, Daylight Computer, and Framework Computer—given the absence of direct public market analogues in this segment.

Stage-progression methodology was the applicable triangulation approach. Using the $40M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement yields an implied valuation of $80M. This reflects the expected equity value expansion between early and mid-stage venture rounds absent material operational or market shifts.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M (low) to $92M (high), with a median point of $80M. The relatively tight band reflects reliance on a single methodological anchor rather than multiple independent approaches. Key sensitivities include: (1) the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier to Whisper Aero's specific stage and capital efficiency relative to peer norms, and (2) the representativeness of the three private comparables given heterogeneity in consumer tech business models and growth profiles. Validation against revenue multiples or venture capital reserve multiples would strengthen conviction, pending availability of operational data from the target and peers.
